setup.title: Set up your admin password
setup.minLength: Password must be at least 8 characters

devices.title: Paired Devices
devices.revokeConfirm: Are you sure you want to revoke {name}? It will no longer be able to access the server.

shares.title: Shared Drives
shares.removeConfirm: Remove {name}? This only removes the share configuration — files on disk are not deleted.
shares.hostPathHint: Absolute path to an existing directory on the server
shares.readOnlyLabel: Read-only (clients cannot upload or modify files)

pairing.title: Device Pairing
pairing.subtitle: Scan this QR code with the Synctuary app to connect a new device.
pairing.noAddress: Could not detect server address. Check network configuration.
pairing.multiInterfaceHint: Multiple network interfaces detected. Select the one your device can reach.
pairing.step1: Open the Synctuary app on your device
pairing.step3: Point your camera at the QR code above
pairing.step4_prefix: Pairing completes automatically — your device will appear in the
pairing.manualHint: If you can't scan the QR code, enter this URL in the app manually:

settings.title: Settings
settings.aboutText: Synctuary is a self-hosted file-sync server for your home LAN.
settings.configHint: Configuration changes beyond what's shown here can be made via the config YAML file or SYNCTUARY_* environment variables. A server restart is required for changes to listen address, TLS, storage paths, and database path.

modeSelector.label: Remote Access Mode
modeSelector.restartBanner: Server restart required for the change to take effect.

disabled.title: Remote Access Disabled
disabled.description: The server is currently accessible only on the local network. To enable remote access, select a mode above and restart the server.
disabled.ipv6Desc: Connect via the server's IPv6 Global Unicast Address. Requires IPv6 connectivity and TLS.
disabled.wgDesc: Built-in VPN tunnel. Clients connect through the standard WireGuard app.

ipv6.title: IPv6 Direct Mode
ipv6.selectGuasHint: Select which GUAs to advertise to clients. Uncheck all to advertise all detected addresses.
ipv6.step1: Ensure your host has a public IPv6 GUA
ipv6.step3: Allow TCP {port} inbound on your router's IPv6 firewall (see below)
ipv6.step4: Use the connection URL above in your Synctuary app
ipv6.windowsFirewallDesc: If the server is running on Windows, add an inbound rule to allow TCP {port}. Run the following in an Administrator PowerShell:
ipv6.windowsFirewallCmd: netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="Synctuary IPv6" dir=in action=allow protocol=TCP localport={port}
ipv6.routerFirewallDesc: Most routers block all inbound IPv6 traffic by default. You need to allow TCP {port} to the server's IPv6 address through the firewall. The exact steps vary by router, but generally:
ipv6.routerStep2: Find the IPv6 firewall or IPv6 packet filter settings
ipv6.routerStep3: Add a rule to allow inbound TCP {port} to the server's IPv6 address shown above
ipv6.routerStep4: If using a whitelist-based filter, add the server address and port as an allowed entry
ipv6.clientRequirementsDesc: The device connecting remotely (e.g. smartphone) must be on a network that supports IPv6. Most major mobile carriers support IPv6, but some Wi-Fi networks or older plans may not. You can verify at test-ipv6.com.

wg.title: WireGuard VPN
wg.noPeers: No peers configured. Add a peer to get started.
wg.step1: Forward UDP {port} on your router to this server
wg.step2: Click "Add Peer" above to generate a client configuration
wg.step3: Import the config into the WireGuard app on your device
wg.step4: Enable the tunnel and connect to {url}
wg.privateKeyWarning: Save this config now. The private key will NOT be shown again.
wg.failedLoad: Failed to load remote access status
